title: A river 'died' overnight in Zambia after an acidic waste spill at a Chinese-owned mine | AFRO American Newspapers
impact_evidence: Environmental | Water Resources | Surface water pollution and quality degradation -> "acid spill at a Chinese-owned mine that contaminated a major river and could potentially affect millions of people after signs of pollution were detected at least 100 kilometers (60 miles) downstream." || Environmental | Land, Soil & Geology | Tailings generation and management -> "The spill happened on Feb. 18 when a tailings dam that holds acidic waste from a copper mine in the north of the country collapsed, according to investigators from the Engineering Institution of Zambia." | "The collapse allowed some 50 million liters of waste containing concentrated acid, dissolved solids and heavy metals to flow into a stream that links to the Kafue River" || Environmental | Water Resources | Groundwater contamination -> "Authorities are concerned that ground water will be contaminated as the mining waste seeps into the earth or is carried to other areas." || Environmental | Biodiversity & Ecosystems | Impacts on aquatic ecosystems and life below water -> "An Associated Press reporter visited parts of the Kafue River, where dead fish could be seen washing up on the banks about 100 kilometers (60 miles) downstream from the mine" | ""Now everything is dead; it's like a totally dead river. impact_evidence: Environmental | Land, Soil & Geology | Tailings generation and management -> "The spill happened on Feb. 18 when a tailings dam that holds acidic waste from a copper mine in the north of the country collapsed, according to investigators from the Engineering Institution of Zambia." || Environmental | Water Resources | Surface water pollution and quality degradation -> "Authorities and environmentalists in Zambia fear the long-term impact of an acid spill at a Chinese-owned mine that contaminated a major river and could potentially affect millions of people after signs of pollution were detected at least 100 kilometers (60 miles) downstream." || Environmental | Water Resources | Groundwater contamination -> "Authorities are concerned that ground water will be contaminated as the mining waste seeps into the earth or is carried to other areas." || Environmental | Water Resources | Freshwater ecotoxicity (heavy metals, pH changes, sensitive species) -> "The collapse allowed some 50 million liters of waste containing concentrated acid, dissolved solids and heavy metals to flow into a stream that links to the Kafue River, Zambia's most important waterway, the engineering institution said." | "An Associated Press reporter visited parts of the Kafue River, where dead fish could be seen washing up on the banks about 100 kilometers (60 miles) downstream from the mine" || Environmental | Biodiversity & Ecosystems | Impacts on aquatic ecosystems and life below water -> "A river 'died' overnight in Zambia after an acidic waste spill at a Chinese-owned mine" | "Now everything is dead, it's like a totally dead river.
